mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS currently at 0.37% indicating deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 persons, signaling weakening labor demand and triggering equity risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 persons
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite payroll deceleration, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, triggering a negative equity risk premium that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 453,438 million dollars, HOUST at 1,089 thousand units
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of joint contraction episodes, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rises above its 3-month moving average, signaling strengthening real economic activity and capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when INDPRO > 3MA are not significantly higher than when INDPRO < 3MA (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating a negative real rate environment that erodes equity valuations through higher inflation risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with monetary accommodation lag
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of high-inflation-low-rates episodes, or the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5%, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity that depresses equity valuations through lower earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when UNRATE > 5.5% are not significantly lower than when UNRATE ≤ 5.5% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) remain below 1,200 thousand units, indicating demand-side weakness that compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 1,200 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,492.7 billion chained 2017 dollars, HOUST at 1,089 thousand units
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of low-growth-low-housing episodes, or the joint interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate analysis over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary supply shock that increases equity risk premium and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of simultaneous INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise episodes, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression over any rolling 12-month window.
